title: The Bed Under You, the Room Around You
subtitle: A visualisation for arriving fully in the present moment before sleep
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation for Day 8 of the sleep arc. The scene is just the room you are in — the bed, the walls, the dark, the quiet. It places the attention in the actual present rather than somewhere the mind has been running.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Built from the Day 8 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795): grounding in the present moment before sleep.

practice.whenToUse: When you are lying in bed but mentally somewhere else — running tomorrow's plans or replaying something from the day.

content.text: A short visualisation for Day 8. Use it lying in bed. The scene is simply the room you're already in — not a beach, not a forest, not somewhere imagined. The practice is about arriving in the actual place you are.
Eyes closed. Picture the room as it is right now.
Start with the bed. The mattress under you. The duvet weight. The specific shape of the pillow under your head. These things are real and present — they're happening right now.
Widen to the room. The walls. The ceiling above you. The corners you can't see in the dark. The window. The door. Hold the shape of the whole room in your mind for a moment.
Notice: you are in this room. Not in the conversation from this afternoon, not in tomorrow's schedule. Here. The room is around you. The bed is under you.
If the mind moves away, to a thought, to tomorrow, to anything outside this room, notice it without effort and bring the picture of the room back. Walls, ceiling, bed, you.
Stay with the room until the room is just the room. This is where you sleep. You are already here.
Original to homemade.education. Built from the Day 8 theme of

(Rebecca J Page, 2025): grounding in the present moment. Present-moment awareness as a sleep aid draws on mindfulness traditions; the room-as-anchor technique is used in various grounding and anxiety-management contexts.
